title: Invalid access token error when calling Zoom API
symptom: When attempting to call the Zoom API for webinar survey results, the request returns 'Authorization failed – Invalid access token'. This indicates that the access token being used is not valid.
applies_when: Using Zoom API with OAuth2 authentication
not_this_if: Access token is valid and correctly configured
Using a different API endpoint that does not require OAuth
root_cause: This error typically occurs when the access token is either expired, not properly generated, or not included in the request headers. It can also happen if the app does not have the necessary permissions or scopes to access the requested resource.
fix_steps: Ensure that you are using the correct OAuth2 flow to obtain a valid access token.
Check that the required scopes are included in your Zoom app settings and that the app is authorized correctly.
Make sure the webinar has ended before trying to access the survey results, as report endpoints populate post-event.
agent_instruction: Verify the OAuth2 setup and ensure the access token is valid before making API calls.
